template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — FEDERAL PLEA AGREEMENT (FED. R. CRIM. P. 11)**:
Draft of a plea agreement for filing in the United States District Court. Follow the conventions used by United States Attorney's Offices — substantively similar across districts, with district-specific local variations. Length: 8–18 pages.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `pleaType`: `11(c)(1)(A)` (government dismisses/won't bring charges) | `11(c)(1)(B)` (government recommends a sentence or range; non-binding) | `11(c)(1)(C)` (binding sentence or range — Court must accept or allow withdrawal).
- `cooperation`: boolean — if true, add USSG § 5K1.1 cooperation framework + 18 U.S.C. § 3553(e) below-mandatory-minimum language.
- `appealWaiver`: boolean — default true; include standard appellate and § 2255 waivers with carve-outs for ineffective-assistance and prosecutorial misconduct.
- `forfeiture`: boolean — include forfeiture allocutions if applicable.

**CAPTION**:

UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT
DISTRICT OF [DISTRICT]

UNITED STATES OF AMERICA,                         )
                                                   )
     v.                                            )    Criminal No.: [XX-CR-XXXXX]
                                                   )
[DEFENDANT NAME],                                  )
                       Defendant.                  )

## PLEA AGREEMENT

Pursuant to Rule 11 of the Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure, the United States of America, by [U.S. Attorney] and [Assistant U.S. Attorney], and the defendant [Name], together with [his/her] attorney [Name], agree as follows:

### 1. THE DEFENDANT'S OBLIGATIONS
(a) The defendant will plead guilty in [District] to Count[s] [X] of the [Information / Indictment] charging [violation — cite U.S.C. section].
(b) The defendant acknowledges that he has reviewed the charging document and the discovery with counsel and understands the nature of the charge and the elements the government would have to prove at trial.

### 2. THE UNITED STATES' OBLIGATIONS
(a) Dismissal: The United States will move at sentencing to dismiss Count[s] [Y, Z] of the [Indictment].
(b) Recommendation: [For 11(c)(1)(B):] The United States will recommend a sentence [of [X] months / within the advisory guideline range / at the low end of the advisory range].
(c) Binding Agreement: [For 11(c)(1)(C):] The parties agree, pursuant to Rule 11(c)(1)(C), that the appropriate disposition is [specific sentence — e.g., 36 months' imprisonment, 3 years' supervised release, restitution in an amount to be determined]. The parties understand that this Court may either ACCEPT this agreement and impose the agreed disposition, or REJECT it (in which case the defendant may withdraw the plea).

### 3. MAXIMUM STATUTORY PENALTIES
The defendant understands that the maximum statutory penalty for the offense of conviction is:
- Imprisonment: [up to X years / not more than Y years].
- Fine: [up to $X / per 18 U.S.C. § 3571].
- Supervised release: [up to X years per 18 U.S.C. § 3583(b)].
- Special assessment: $100 per felony count per 18 U.S.C. § 3013.
- Restitution: mandatory under 18 U.S.C. § 3663A (MVRA) for covered offenses.

### 4. SENTENCING GUIDELINES
(a) The parties stipulate to the following Guidelines calculations, subject to the Court's independent determination:
   - Base Offense Level: [X] [USSG § ____].
   - Specific Offense Characteristics: [list with cites].
   - Adjustments: [Role § 3B; Obstruction § 3C; Acceptance § 3E1.1(a) and (b)].
   - Total Offense Level: [X].
   - Criminal History Category: [I–VI] [based on USSG Chapter 4].
   - Advisory Guideline Range: [XX–YY months].
(b) The parties understand that the Guidelines are advisory only. United States v. Booker, 543 U.S. 220 (2005). The Court must consider the § 3553(a) factors. United States v. Gall, 552 U.S. 38 (2007).

### 5. ACCEPTANCE OF RESPONSIBILITY (USSG § 3E1.1)
The United States agrees that, absent further obstructive conduct, the defendant has clearly demonstrated acceptance of responsibility and is entitled to a two-level reduction under § 3E1.1(a). Where offense level is 16 or greater and the defendant timely notifies authorities of the intent to plead guilty, the United States will move for an additional one-level reduction under § 3E1.1(b).

### 6. COOPERATION [if applicable]
The defendant agrees to cooperate fully with the United States, including by providing truthful and complete information and testifying at any trial or proceeding. Should the defendant provide substantial assistance, the United States may in its sole discretion file a motion under USSG § 5K1.1 for a departure below the advisory Guideline range and, where applicable, under 18 U.S.C. § 3553(e) for a sentence below any mandatory minimum. Whether the defendant's assistance is substantial is left to the sole and non-reviewable discretion of the United States.

### 7. APPELLATE AND POST-CONVICTION WAIVERS [if applicable]
(a) The defendant knowingly and voluntarily waives the right to appeal the conviction and any sentence imposed, EXCEPT: (i) a sentence above the agreed or advisory Guideline range; (ii) claims of ineffective assistance of counsel; or (iii) prosecutorial misconduct.
(b) The defendant knowingly and voluntarily waives any right to challenge the conviction or sentence under 28 U.S.C. § 2255, EXCEPT for claims of ineffective assistance of counsel or prosecutorial misconduct.

### 8. FORFEITURE [if applicable]
The defendant agrees to forfeit [specific property] pursuant to [21 U.S.C. § 853 / 18 U.S.C. § 981 / other forfeiture statute]. The defendant waives any claim to the forfeited property, waives notice and hearing, and agrees to the entry of a Preliminary Order of Forfeiture.

### 9. RESTITUTION
The parties agree that restitution in an amount to be determined by the Court is [mandatory under the MVRA / discretionary] and that the defendant will make restitution to the victim(s).

### 10. BREACH
If the defendant breaches this Agreement, the United States may, in its sole discretion: (a) declare the Agreement null and void; (b) prosecute the defendant on any dismissed or uncharged counts; and (c) use any statements or information provided by the defendant against the defendant. The defendant waives the protections of Fed. R. Evid. 410 and Fed. R. Crim. P. 11(f) with respect to any subsequent prosecution.

### 11. INTEGRATION
This is the complete and integrated agreement between the United States and the defendant. Any modification must be in writing and signed by the parties.

### 12. VOLUNTARINESS
The defendant acknowledges that [he/she] has read this Agreement, reviewed it with counsel, and enters into it knowingly and voluntarily.

---

Dated: ________________

_______________________________          _______________________________
[Defendant]                               [Assistant U.S. Attorney]

_______________________________
[Defense Counsel], Esq. (BBO #/Bar #)

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Every plea agreement is an OFFER until the Court accepts the plea. Use drafting that reflects this.
- Cite Rule 11(c)(1)(A), (B), or (C) correctly — the distinction is consequential for withdrawal rights.
- Include § 3553(a) Booker/Gall advisory-only language.
- Mandatory minimums are not overridden by a plea agreement EXCEPT via § 3553(e) / § 5K1.1 motion by the government.
- Appellate waivers must include the standard ineffective-assistance carve-out.
- MVRA restitution is MANDATORY for covered offenses — do not agree to waive it.
hints: Federal plea agreement under Fed. R. Crim. P. 11. Three 11(c)(1) variants: (A) government drops/won't bring; (B) non-binding recommendation; (C) binding sentence with withdrawal rights. Sentencing Guidelines advisory after Booker (Gall reaffirms). USSG § 3E1.1 acceptance of responsibility (2-level + 1-level). USSG § 5K1.1 / 18 U.S.C. § 3553(e) cooperation. Standard appellate and § 2255 waivers with IAC + prosecutorial-misconduct carve-outs. MVRA restitution mandatory. Fed. R. Evid. 410 / Rule 11(f) waiver on breach.
